questions.q: Which of the following is a requirement for preparing a cash flow statement?
opts: (A) All the transactions should be recorded in the cash book
(B) Financial statements should be prepared in compliance with the accounting standards
(C) All the non-cash items should be ignored while preparing the cash flow statement
(D) Financial statements should be audited before preparing the cash flow statement
questions.explanation: The cash flow statement should be prepared from the cash book which is a record of all the transactions. It is a requirement for preparing a cash flow statement.

questions.q: What is the primary purpose of the reconciliation of profit computed on the basis of financial statements under the cash basis of accounting with the profit computed on the basis of financial statements under the accrual basis of accounting?
opts: (A) To find out the difference between two methods of accounting
(B) To determine the profitability of the business
(C) To compare the financial performance of two businesses
(D) To identify the reasons for differences in profit computed on the basis of cash and accrual basis
questions.explanation: The primary purpose of reconciliation is to identify the reasons for differences in profit computed on the basis of cash and accrual basis. This helps in understanding the financial performance of the business.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a good analysis of financial statements?
opts: (A) It should be based on the historical data
(B) It should be based on the subjective judgment of the analyst
(C) It should be prepared with the help of computer software
(D) It should be based on the comparison of financial statements with the industry standards and benchmarks
questions.explanation: A good analysis of financial statements should be based on the comparison of financial statements with the industry standards and benchmarks. This helps in understanding the financial performance of the business in relation to its peers.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the statement of changes in equity?
opts: (A) To show the changes in the retained earnings
(B) To show the changes in the equity of the company
(C) To show the changes in the liabilities of the company
(D) To show the changes in the assets of the company
questions.explanation: The statement of changes in equity shows the changes in the equity of the company, including the changes in the retained earnings, dividends, and others.

questions.q: Which of the following is a limitation of the ratio analysis?
opts: (A) It is based on the historical data
(B) It is not based on the subjective judgment of the analyst
(C) It is based on the comparison of financial statements with the industry standards and benchmarks
(D) It does not consider the qualitative factors
questions.explanation: The ratio analysis does not consider the qualitative factors, such as the management's efficiency, market conditions, and others. This is a limitation of the ratio analysis.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the trend analysis?
opts: (A) To show the changes in the financial statements over a period of time
(B) To compare the financial statements with the industry standards and benchmarks
(C) To identify the strengths and weaknesses of the business
(D) To predict the future financial performance of the business
questions.explanation: The trend analysis is used to predict the future financial performance of the business by analyzing the past trends and patterns in the financial statements.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a good financial analyst?
opts: (A) He should be a chartered accountant
(B) He should have experience in the financial industry
(C) He should be able to analyze the financial statements quickly and accurately
(D) He should be able to prepare the financial statements
questions.explanation: A good financial analyst should be able to analyze the financial statements quickly and accurately. This requires a strong understanding of accounting and finance principles, as well as analytical skills.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the horizontal analysis?
opts: (A) To compare the financial statements with the industry standards and benchmarks
(B) To show the changes in the financial statements over a period of time
(C) To identify the strengths and weaknesses of the business
(D) To predict the future financial performance of the business
questions.explanation: The horizontal analysis is used to show the changes in the financial statements over a period of time. It helps in understanding the trends and patterns in the financial statements.

questions.q: Which of the following is a limitation of the horizontal analysis?
opts: (A) It is based on the historical data
(B) It is not based on the subjective judgment of the analyst
(C) It is based on the comparison of financial statements with the industry standards and benchmarks
(D) It does not consider the qualitative factors
questions.explanation: The horizontal analysis does not consider the qualitative factors, such as the management's efficiency, market conditions, and others. This is a limitation of the horizontal analysis.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the vertical analysis?
opts: (A) To compare the financial statements with the industry standards and benchmarks
(B) To show the changes in the financial statements over a period of time
(C) To identify the strengths and weaknesses of the business
(D) To show the relationship between different items in the financial statements
questions.explanation: The vertical analysis is used to show the relationship between different items in the financial statements. It helps in understanding the composition of the financial statements.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a good vertical analysis?
opts: (A) It should be based on the historical data
(B) It should be based on the subjective judgment of the analyst
(C) It should be prepared with the help of computer software
(D) It should be based on the comparison of financial statements with the industry standards and benchmarks
questions.explanation: A good vertical analysis should be based on the comparison of financial statements with the industry standards and benchmarks. This helps in understanding the composition of the financial statements in relation to its peers.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the common-size statement?
opts: (A) To show the changes in the financial statements over a period of time
(B) To compare the financial statements with the industry standards and benchmarks
(C) To identify the strengths and weaknesses of the business
(D) To show the relationship between different items in the financial statements
questions.explanation: The common-size statement is used to show the relationship between different items in the financial statements. It helps in understanding the composition of the financial statements.

opts: (A) It is based on the historical data
(B) It is not based on the subjective judgment of the analyst
(C) It is based on the comparison of financial statements with the industry standards and benchmarks
(D) It does not consider the qualitative factors
questions.explanation: The common-size statement does not consider the qualitative factors, such as the management's efficiency, market conditions, and others. This is a limitation of the common-size statement.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the ratio analysis?
opts: (A) To show the changes in the financial statements over a period of time
(B) To compare the financial statements with the industry standards and benchmarks
(C) To identify the strengths and weaknesses of the business
(D) To evaluate the financial performance and position of a business
questions.explanation: The ratio analysis is used to evaluate the financial performance and position of a business. It helps in understanding the financial health and sustainability of the business.

questions.explanation: A good ratio analysis should be based on the comparison of financial statements with the industry standards and benchmarks. This helps in understanding the financial performance and position of the business in relation to its peers.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the DuPont analysis?
opts: (A) To show the changes in the financial statements over a period of time
(B) To compare the financial statements with the industry standards and benchmarks
(C) To identify the strengths and weaknesses of the business
(D) To evaluate the return on equity of a business
questions.explanation: The DuPont analysis is used to evaluate the return on equity of a business. It helps in understanding the financial performance and position of the business.

questions.explanation: The DuPont analysis does not consider the qualitative factors, such as the management's efficiency, market conditions, and others. This is a limitation of the DuPont analysis.
